title: Maze Graphs, Connectivity, Cycles, Components, and Trees · Program Brief: Graph Complexity
summary: The solver treats walls as edges, generation visits display objects, disconnected cells are hidden, and tree claims rely on appearance. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the graph complexity increment in validated maze graph adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State vertices, edges, representation, traversal work, memory, and grid-specific bounds.
Establish connectedness plus acyclicity or the equivalent finite undirected edge condition before calling a maze perfect.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 5504 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate graph mapping, connectivity, components, cycles, and tree claims; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: State vertices, edges, representation, traversal work, memory, and grid-specific bounds. A defensible solution applies Graph Complexity, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to validated maze graph adapter.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-graph-topology-4
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-graph-topology-4.
# Case 1655: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def worked_maze_graph_topology_4(vertices: int = 48, edges: int = 47, reachable: int = 48, components: int = 1, cycle_witnesses: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "gridworked4",
        "topology1655",
        "search4dekrow",
        "eventworked41655",
        "access1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if vertices < 1 or edges < 0:
        return False, vertices, "graph-count"
    if reachable != vertices or components != 1:
        return False, reachable, "connectivity"
    if cycle_witnesses > 0:
        return False, cycle_witnesses, "cycle-detected"
    if edges != vertices - 1:
        return False, edges, "tree-edge-count"
    assert edges == vertices - 1 and reachable == vertices
    return True, reachable, "validated-maze-tree"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State vertices, edges, representation, traversal work, memory, and grid-specific bounds.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Graph Complexity model · Theory Graph Complexity, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 1170 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate graph mapping, connectivity, components, cycles, and tree claims; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: State vertices, edges, representation, traversal work, memory, and grid-specific bounds.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Graph Complexity model · Theory Graph Complexity, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Algorithm complexity can be reported without defining graph representation.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-graph-complexity-1gg3hy(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_graph_complexity_1gg3hy\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?vertices < 1 or edges < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reachable != vertices or components != 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cycle_witnesses > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?edges != vertices - 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
